methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Thunder Tiger operates in the Defense & Security sector as a public company, having raised $22M+ as of 2025 under CEO Gene Su. The valuation analysis draws from a broader Defense & Security comp universe of 21 same-sector peers, including two public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and eight private peers such as Anduril Industries, Scale AI, Saronic, Flock Safety, and Shield AI.

Peer-median methodology proved inapplicable due to insufficient narrow peer comparables. Despite 21 same-sector companies existing, zero peers met simultaneous filters for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier classification. This constraint prevents reliable extraction of median revenue multiples, growth rates, or valuation benchmarks from a sufficiently comparable subset. A broad all-sector median would introduce material bias and is therefore not deployed.

The analysis cannot produce a triangulated valuation range without sufficient quantitative data on peer financials, multiples, or precedent transactions. Key uncertainties constraining valuation include the absence of standardized financial disclosures across the private comp set, limited public company data points in this subsector, and the nascent maturity of Defense & Security frontier-tech valuations. To advance analysis, additional datapoints on peer revenue, EBITDA margins, growth rates, or recent funding rounds would be required to establish defensible multiples-based or comparable-transaction benchmarks.
